case.paper_title: A Business by Any Other Name: Firm Name Choice as a Signal of Firm Quality
case.focal_claim: The average plumbing firm whose name begins with A or a number receives more service complaints than other firms.

original_datasets.name: final_data

summary_statistics.info: DataFrame with 2293 rows and 19 columns. Types: firm_id (object), others float32. Key variables: complaints_2008, first_A, num_names_2008.

original_datasets.notes: Dataset appears to be Illinois plumbing firms linked across sources. Some pricing/review variables observed for a subset (e.g., 543 observations). Primary analysis uses complaints_2008, first_A, and num_names_2008.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Regress standardized total complaints on first_A indicator and standardized number of names used by the firm.
preprocessing_steps: Ensure complaints_2008, first_A, and num_names_2008 are numeric.
Drop rows with missing values in required variables.

key_choices.outcome_measure: Overall BBB complaints in 2008 (complaints_2008), standardized for modeling.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Indicator for name begins with 'A' or a number (first_A).
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop rows with missing values in complaints_2008, first_A, or num_names_2008.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Total number of BBB complaints in 2008 for the firm.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator equal to 1 if firm name begins with 'A' or a number; 0 otherwise.

controls.name: num_names_2008
controls.definition: Number of different names used by the firm in 2008.

analysis_code.notes: Script expects final_data.dta at /app/data. Outputs JSON summary to /app/data/task1_results.json.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Use only the data of Illinois plumbing firms; do not restrict to metro Chicago; do not include review-site data; use overall complaints number (not per-employee).

analysis_path.path_description: Regress standardized total complaints on first_A and standardized number of names; report F-test (equivalently t^2) and p-value for first_A.
preprocessing_steps: Ensure complaints_2008, first_A, and num_names_2008 are numeric.
Drop rows with missing values in required variables.

key_choices.outcome_measure: Overall BBB complaints in 2008 (complaints_2008), standardized for modeling.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Indicator for name begins with 'A' or a number (first_A).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Use entire Illinois plumbing firms dataset; do not restrict to metro Chicago.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop rows with missing values in complaints_2008, first_A, or num_names_2008.
key_choices.inference_rule: F-test (equivalently t^2) and p-value for first_A coefficient.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Total number of BBB complaints in 2008 for the firm.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator equal to 1 if firm name begins with 'A' or a number; 0 otherwise.

controls.name: num_names_2008
controls.definition: Number of different names used by the firm in 2008.

analysis_code.notes: Script expects final_data.dta at /app/data. Outputs JSON summary with F-statistic to /app/data/task2_results.json.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned linear regression path with F-test for first_A; do not substitute other outcomes or samples.

files.data/analysis_task1__py.py: Python rewrite of Task1. Loads /app/data/final_data.dta, standardizes complaints_2008 and num_names_2008, runs OLS: y_z ~ first_A + num_names_z, outputs JSON.
files.data/analysis_task2__py.py: Python rewrite of Task2. Same model as Task1, additionally reports F-test (t^2) for first_A, outputs JSON.

codebase.notes: Original R scripts (data/analysis_task1.R, data/analysis_task2.R) are present but not executed per run policy. Ensure final_data.dta is mounted to /app/data at runtime.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset final_data.dta mounted at /app/data. Produce JSON outputs with coefficients, test statistics, p-values, and model fit.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Coefficient and p-value for first_A; coefficient and p-value for num_names_2008; F-statistic or t-statistic for first_A; R-squared; number of observations.
